description_long: This is a modern, three-seat bench with a sleek, utilitarian design, commonly found in public waiting areas such as bus stations, airports, or urban parks. The bench features three individual seats, each with a slightly curved backrest and seat pan for ergonomic support. The frame and legs are constructed from sturdy metal, likely steel or aluminum, with a dark finish and silver accent bars along the seat and backrest edges. The legs are wide and flat, providing stability and distributing weight evenly, and are connected by a horizontal support bar beneath the seats. The seat and backrest surfaces appear to be made of a durable, hard material, possibly powder-coated metal or high-density plastic, designed for heavy use and easy cleaning. The overall aesthetic is minimalist and functional, with no armrests and a focus on durability and ease of maintenance.
description: A modern, three-seat bench with curved backrests and a sturdy metal frame, designed for public waiting areas. The bench features a minimalist, durable construction with dark surfaces and silver accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing three individual seats with curved backrests and a robust metal frame.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved profile of the seat and backrest, as well as the wide, flat leg design.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the continuous back panel and the horizontal support bar connecting the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the seat curvature and leg structure.
attribute_leg_design: wide flat metal supports with horizontal connecting bar
